name: voltageDropCalc
_note: Pure compute, reads artifact.data (NecCodeCalc runDomain wrapper, peeled at dispatch). 1-phase drop = (R/1000)×ft×A×2 (3-phase ×1.732); % = drop/voltage×100. Fail-closed: non-finite amps/distance/voltage sanitise to safe defaults via num() (15/100/120, never zero) so dropPercentValue is always finite. acceptable = drop ≤ 3% (NEC branch-circuit limit). RECOMMENDATION FIX (2026-06-28): the upgrade now points at the next HEAVIER gauge (lower AWG = thicker = less drop) and gives honest advice whenever drop > 3% — the prior code walked toward THINNER wire and printed 'within acceptable limits' on a 30% drop, a dangerous lie for a safety calc. Cards render voltageDrop/dropPercent/acceptable/wireGauge/recommendation/necLimit/distance/current/voltage EXACTLY (field-aligned against VoltageDropChart). No STATE, no DB.
